input.background: The problem involves estimating the standard deviation using the mean range, understanding the skewness and kurtosis coefficients of a random variable, and the concept of the median in a dataset. Skewness measures the symmetry of a distribution, while kurtosis measures the heaviness of the tails of the distribution. The median is the central value in an ordered dataset.
input.question: Explain the concept of the median in a dataset and how it is calculated when \( n \) (the total number of observations) is even.

output.answer: The median is the central point of a series when the data are arranged in order of magnitude, i.e., there are equal numbers of observations greater than and less than the median. When \( n \) is even, it is usual to take the mean of the two central values as the median.

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
